items.prompt: What makes a complexity explanation defensible?

options.text: It identifies the input variables, cost model, executed operations, and how their counts compose.
It names a familiar Big-O class without justification.
It counts loop keywords.
It interprets variable names as complexity annotations.

feedback.reason: A good explanation connects program behavior to an asymptotic expression.
feedback.details: It also states whether the bound is worst-case, expected, amortized, or output-sensitive.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.label_only: A label alone does not show why it applies.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.syntax_only: Loop syntax can misrepresent aggregate work.

items.prompt: Why is "there are two nested loops, so the algorithm is O(n²)" incomplete?

options.text: The inner bounds and whether work resets or is globally bounded must be analyzed.
Nested loops can never be quadratic.
Two loops always add to O(n).
The loop variables may not be named i and j.

feedback.reason: Nested loops multiply only when the inner work repeats independently for each outer iteration.
feedback.details: Two-pointer and amortized patterns can have nested syntax with linear total work.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.nested_never: Independent full scans often are quadratic.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.two_means_linear: That is true only for sequential loops.

items.prompt: A report says a function is O(log n) because it contains a variable named mid. What is the defect?

options.text: It does not show that the remaining domain shrinks geometrically or that midpoint work is bounded.
The name mid is reserved for O(n) algorithms.
Every midpoint computation is logarithmic by definition.
The name affects space but not time.

feedback.reason: Logarithmic time comes from a geometric reduction in candidates.
feedback.details: The explanation must derive iteration count from boundary updates.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.log_always: The updates may shrink by one or not at all.

items.prompt: A query method is described as O(1), but building its lookup table costs O(n). What should the full one-query workload report?

options.text: O(n) total for build plus one O(1) query, unless preprocessing already exists outside the measured workload.
O(1) unconditionally.
O(n²).
O(k) for an unrelated output size.

feedback.reason: Complexity boundaries must match the workload boundary.
feedback.details: Per-query cost is valid only after clearly excluding or amortizing preprocessing.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.output: The stated workload returns one query result.

items.prompt: A function returns k records but claims O(1) time because the records were already known. What is missing?

options.text: The cost of materializing or emitting the k required records.
Nothing; known values require no output work.
A variable must be named output.
All output is automatically amortized.

feedback.reason: A constant-time discovery does not imply constant-time materialization.
feedback.details: The result contract imposes an Ω(k) output lower bound.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.search_only: The interface still has to produce them.

items.prompt: A dynamic-array append is labeled worst-case O(1) because its amortized cost is O(1). What is the correction?

options.text: A resize-triggering append can be O(n) worst-case even though a sequence of appends is O(1) amortized each.
Worst-case and amortized must always be equal.
Every append copies the whole array.
Amortized means average over random inputs.

feedback.reason: Amortized throughput does not imply a per-call latency bound.
feedback.details: The explanation should state both costs when the distinction matters.

items.prompt: Why is a library call not automatically O(1)?

options.text: The call may sort, scan, copy, allocate, or hash data proportional to input size.
All library calls are O(n²).
Parentheses make a call constant-time.
Compiled code has no asymptotic cost.

feedback.reason: Abstraction hides implementation details, not computational cost.
feedback.details: A complexity explanation must use the documented or derived cost of the called operation.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.syntax: Syntax does not reveal implementation work.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.compiled: Compilation does not remove runtime operations.

items.prompt: Two sequential loops each scan n elements. Why is O(n²) the wrong explanation?

options.text: Their costs add to 2n rather than multiplying.
Loops cost nothing when sequential.
Only the first loop counts.
The loops use different variable names.

feedback.reason: Multiplication applies to repeated nesting, while sequential phases add.
feedback.details: Dropping the constant factor gives O(n).

items.prompt: A space explanation sums every temporary buffer ever allocated, even though their lifetimes do not overlap. What is the correction?

options.text: Use peak simultaneously live memory, taking a maximum for nonoverlapping buffers.
Keep the sum because allocation count is time.
Count only the final buffer regardless of size.
Temporary buffers are output.

feedback.reason: Space complexity measures the largest live footprint.
feedback.details: Lifetime analysis determines whether terms add or take a maximum.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.time_sum: Space and cumulative allocation are different.

items.prompt: A recursive algorithm makes two child calls, and the explanation counts only recursion depth. What may be missing from time analysis?

options.text: The total number of calls across the recursion tree and local work per call.
Stack depth is always the complete time bound.
The child function names.
Only output size can affect recursion.

feedback.reason: Time counts every executed call, while stack space counts simultaneous depth.
feedback.details: Confusing the two can turn an exponential tree into a falsely linear explanation.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.stack_only_time: Depth measures one path, not all branches.

items.prompt: Which elements belong in a strong complexity explanation? Select all that apply.

options.text: The input-size variables and their relationships.
The assumed cost of primitive or library operations.
How sequential, nested, recursive, and output work combine.
The semantic meaning inferred from names such as i, mid, or result.

feedback.reason: A complexity label should be reproducible from stated assumptions and executed work.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.variable_names: Names are not an operation count.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.cost_model: Operation cost may depend on width or data structure.

items.prompt: Which are common missing terms in complexity reports? Select all that apply.

options.text: A sort, index build, or prefix-table construction.
The number q multiplying per-query cost.
The cost and storage of k returned records.
The number of comments in source code.

feedback.reason: Reports often quote the cheapest visible phase and omit the rest of the workload.
feedback.details: A full expression should match the requested lifecycle.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.preprocessing: One-time work may dominate small workloads.

items.prompt: Which statements are false heuristics? Select all that apply.

options.text: Nested loops are always O(n²).
Using a midpoint makes time logarithmic.
Amortized O(1) equals worst-case O(1).
Sequential phases add their operation counts.

feedback.reason: Complexity must be derived from execution, not memorized visual patterns.
feedback.details: The sequential-add rule is sound when all phases execute.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sequential_add: This is a valid composition rule.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.nested_quadratic: Aggregate bounds and asymmetric dimensions can differ.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.amortized_worst: An individual operation may be expensive.

items.prompt: Which review questions help detect an invalid output-sensitive claim? Select all that apply.

options.text: How many records can the contract require?
How much representation each result requires.
Whether outputs are stored, streamed, or represented symbolically under the contract.
Whether the output loop is named emit.

feedback.reason: Output lower bounds depend on result count, representation, and interface.
feedback.details: A symbolic or lazy interface should not be confused with mandatory materialization.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.loop_name: Names do not change emission work.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.record_size: A result may contain multiple elements.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.materialized_or_streamed: The interface affects space and timing.

items.prompt: Which statements correctly separate time and space reasoning? Select all that apply.

options.text: Time counts cumulative executed work, while space usually counts peak live storage.
Recursive time may depend on all calls, while stack space depends on maximum active depth.
Output storage can be reported separately from auxiliary space.
Time and space complexities must have the same class.

feedback.reason: A sound explanation uses the correct aggregation rule for each resource.
feedback.details: The same execution can have high time and low auxiliary space or vice versa.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.calls_vs_depth: Branching and depth answer different questions.

items.prompt: Arrange the construction of a concise complexity explanation.

elements.text: State input variables, case, and primitive-operation assumptions.
Explain how many times dominant operations execute.
Include preprocessing, repeated queries, output, and peak storage where applicable.
Simplify the expression and report time, auxiliary space, and output space separately.

feedback.reason: The explanation moves from model to operations to complete workload to final notation.
feedback.details: This order makes hidden assumptions visible.

items.prompt: A function sorts n records, scans them once, and returns k copied records. Select time, auxiliary space excluding the sort implementation and output, and output space.
constraints: Comparison sorting costs O(n log n).
The scan and each copied output record cost O(1).
The chosen sort uses O(1) additional working storage for this question.

feedback.reason: The sort dominates the input-processing term, and k output copies add output-sensitive work.
feedback.details: The result list is output rather than auxiliary storage.

items.prompt: A dynamic array receives n appends with geometric resizing. Select total sequence cost, amortized cost per append, and worst-case cost of one append.
constraints: Copying one stored element is O(1).

feedback.reason: A complete report keeps total, amortized, and per-operation worst case distinct.
feedback.details: Conflating them is the exact mistake this review unit is designed to catch.
